path_scroll.title: Find the Path to Divinity scroll — not a standard scroll. It gives a Corrupted Knowledge book. Do not discard it.

know_cost.title: Understand the cost — 999 souls via Mass Accumulate, Homunculus chain, Philosopher's Stone at the end

subtitle.text: The scroll is corrupted. That is not a flaw. It was always going to look like this.

description: CH.12 · PATH TO DIVINITY
▸ The Path to Divinity scroll is not a normal scroll.
When researched, it gives a Corrupted Knowledge book
called 'Path to Divinity'.
The book says: 'Do not keep this.'
Keep it.
◈ WHAT PATH TO DIVINITY IS
└ Divine mod (dvine: / ptd-1.0.5-forge-1.20.1.jar)
adds the Philosopher's Stone mechanic
└ The path to godhood requires:
Step 1 — Brew the Homunculus
Black Fluid + Eye of Ender (brewing stand)
→ Homunculus
Step 2 — Activate the Soul Bar
Use the Homunculus → soul HUD appears
Kill mobs to fill the soul bar
Step 3 — Manifest the Philosopher's Stone
Press M when soul bar full (999 souls)
→ Philosopher's Stone manifested as armor skin
└ Status: divine player has integrity bar
0 integrity + any damage = divine status lost
◈ MASS ACCUMULATE — THE COST
└ To fill the soul bar quickly: Mass Accumulate ability
└ This requires killing 999 villagers
└ The pack does not prevent this
It records it.
Other mechanics respond to it.
└ `/divinecheck` — check if a player is divine
◈ DIVINE STATUS EFFECTS
└ Philosopher's Stone active: stat buffs + glowing skin
└ Force-counter: other divine player can press M to
instantly drop your integrity to 0
└ In multiplayer: divine players can counter each other
└ In singleplayer: divine status is permanent until damage
depletes integrity
▸ The Corrupted Knowledge book says: 'Do not keep this.'
You are reading the quest.
You know what the book contains.
The decision has already been made.
Keep it or don't.
The nine civilizations made the same choice.
Look at what they built.
▶ Find the Path to Divinity scroll [check]
▶ Understand what the 999 cost means [check]
◇ The Manifestor Trap happened before you arrived.
Path to Divinity is what happens after.
Both are a transaction.
The question is what you get and what gets you.
